artifacts.content:
Hi Isa,

Finally catching a quiet moment while Mom dozes, so I wanted to give you a fuller download on how things are going and what the next few weeks might look like.

Since her complications after the 1/15 surgery at Swedish, I’ve basically been camping out at Harbor Ave every day. I’m the one managing her oxycodone doses every 4 hours and helping her with bathroom transfers so she doesn’t fall. The surgeon’s note from 1/22/26 literally says she can’t be left alone for more than 30 minutes at a time for the next 4–6 weeks because of the dizziness, blood pressure swings, and fall risk from the anesthesia mess, so I’m trying to take that seriously.

Practically, it means I’m working from my laptop at her dining table, bouncing between Jira tickets and making sure she gets to the bathroom without face-planting. I warned my team that I may need to step away from Teams a couple of times a day for “personal caregiver stuff,” which mostly means shower help and checking that left hip incision. Her Swedish doc wants daily photos of the incision through mid-February so they can watch for infection, so I’ve added “amateur medical photographer” to my resume.

I’m also lining up a part-time home health aide to cover the 3–6 p.m. window starting the week of 2/3, since Medicare would only approve two short visits a week. Between that and the January co-pays I’ve already put about $3,200 on my credit card, so I’m really trying to avoid going down the unpaid leave route and instead keep my full hours remote.

How are things on your end? How’s Sofia doing with school and all this grown-up drama swirling in the background?

Love,
Michael

To: Karen L. Whitfield
From: Michael J. Ramirez
Date: 2026-01-22 10:37 a.m.
Subject: Request for Temporary Remote Work Arrangement Due to Family Medical Situation

Dear Ms. Whitfield,

I am writing to formally request a temporary remote work arrangement due to an unexpected family medical situation that requires my increased presence at home over the next several weeks.

By way of context, my mother, Elena Ramirez (age 71), experienced complications following a minor surgery on 2026-01-15. While she is stable and recovering, her mobility is significantly limited, and she has been advised by her physician not to remain at home alone for extended periods for at least the next 4–6 weeks. As her primary caregiver living closest to her (I am approximately 20 minutes away from her apartment in West Seattle), I need to be available throughout the day to assist with medication management, physical therapy exercises, meal preparation, and general supervision to ensure she does not fall or overexert herself.

If there are any questions about my availability, I’m happy to share the note from her surgeon at Swedish dated 01/22/2026 that explicitly says she can’t be left alone for more than 30 minutes at a time for the next 4–6 weeks because of her fall risk, dizziness, and blood pressure issues after the anesthesia complications.

I want to emphasize that my request is not to reduce my working hours or responsibilities, but rather to change my primary work location for a defined period so that I can better balance my job duties with these temporary family obligations. I remain fully committed to my role and to meeting all project deadlines and performance expectations.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

Michael J. Ramirez
Senior Software Engineer, Payments & Billing Team
NorthBridge Technologies

metadata.subject: Request for Temporary Remote Work Arrangement Due to Family Medical Situation

artifacts.content:
Title: Caregiving + work logistics – Feb 2026

1. Mom’s medical constraints
- Post-op from 01/15/2026 Swedish surgery (West Seattle campus).
- Surgeon’s 01/22/2026 note: she can’t be left alone >30 minutes at a time for 4–6 weeks due to dizziness, BP issues, and high fall risk.
- Oxycodone dosing every 4 hours – set alarms (6a, 10a, 2p, 6p, 10p) when I’m at Harbor.
- Help with bathroom transfers; watch for signs she’s overexerting.
- Daily photos of left hip incision through mid-Feb 2026 per Swedish doc; upload to MyChart.

2. Coverage plan
- I’m at Harbor Ave most weekdays, working from the dining table.
- Part-time home health aide covering 3–6 p.m. starting week of 02/03/2026; I’m paying out of pocket since Medicare only covers two short visits/week.
- Track costs: already ~$3,200 on credit card (Jan surgery co-pays + aide deposit).

3. Work boundaries
- Keep strict 9–5:30 schedule; log on from home office on 38th when I can.
- Update Teams status when stepping away for showers/meds/incision photos (probably a couple times a day).
- If I suddenly drop from a meeting, could be BP crash like 01/28/2026; need to call Swedish cardiology nurse line and maybe get her back to ER at Cherry Hill.

4. Self-care / Emily
- Rotate evenings at home vs. Harbor so Emily and I still get some normal time with Oscar.
- Remember: ask Emily which nights she can swing by Harbor so I can get a real break.

Thread: Tuesday check-in

Michael: Hey, just a heads up before my day gets away from me.

Michael: If I randomly disappear from Teams or drop from a Zoom this week, it’s probably Mom-related, not me rage-quitting sprint planning.

Emily: Got it. How is she this morning?

Michael: Better-ish. Still wobbly. I’m juggling Jira and her oxycodone timer – dosing every 4 hours while I’m here at Harbor.

Michael: Her BP was weird again last night and I kept flashing back to 1/28 when it crashed and we ended up at Swedish Cherry Hill ER. I’ve got the cardiology nurse line on speed dial and my phone on loud even when I’m at the 38th Ave office.

Emily: Ugh, I hate that night. Do you feel okay doing calls from there?

Michael: Mostly. I just warn people I may need to step away a couple times a day to help her in and out of the shower or take the daily pic of the left hip incision. Swedish wants those through mid-Feb so they can watch for infection.

Emily: That’s a lot. Anything I can take off your plate this week?

Michael: If you can do Oscar’s evening walk tonight and maybe swing by Mom’s tomorrow around 5, that’d be huge. Home health aide starts covering 3–6 p.m. next week (week of 2/3), but until then it’s just me.

Emily: I can do both. Text me if you end up at Cherry Hill again.

Michael: Will do. Love you.

Emily: Love you too.

Subject: Weekly plan for Dad’s rehab + Mom’s stuff

Hey Em,

Putting this in writing so we’re on the same page for the next couple months. Between Dad’s post-stroke rehab runs to Tacoma and Mom’s recovery here, my calendar looks like a murder board.

Dad: I’m planning to be in Tacoma 3–4 days a week through June, splitting time between Tacoma General (therapy sessions) and his apartment. I’ll mostly do early drives down, work from his place between PT/OT blocks, then head back after dinner. I’ll keep Tuesdays lighter because of your Harborview therapy slot from 3–5 p.m. – I still want to be the one who drives you there and back if we can swing it.

Mom: Status quo for now – Harbor Ave most weekdays, juggling her 4-hour oxy schedule, bathroom transfers, and the daily left hip incision photo Swedish wants through mid-Feb. Surgeon’s 1/22 note still says she can’t be alone for more than 30 minutes for 4–6 weeks, so I’m trying not to cheat that.

Money: Between Mom’s January co-pays and paying out of pocket for the 3–6 p.m. home health aide starting week of 2/3, I’m already at about $3,200 on my credit card. Add gas and parking for Tacoma General and Harborview and it’s… a lot. Another reason I’m clinging to full-time remote instead of any unpaid leave.

Can we look at the calendar tonight and mark:
- Your Harborview Tuesdays
- My Tacoma days
- Which evenings you can cover Harbor Ave so I can have a real night off?

Thanks for tag-teaming all of this with me.

M

metadata.subject: Weekly plan for Dad’s rehab + Mom’s stuff

artifacts.content:

Date: Wednesday, 2026-02-05
Time: 8:00 a.m. – 5:30 p.m.
Location: Mom’s apartment – 1420 Harbor Ave SW, Apt 504, Seattle, WA
Attendees: Michael (required)

Description/Notes:

Plan:
- 7:30–8:00: Drive from 38th Ave SW to Harbor Ave; quick Oscar walk before leaving.
- 8:00–9:00: Emails + code review for Payments & Billing.
- 9:00–9:15: Daily stand-up (Teams).
- 9:15–11:00: Heads-down on retry/idempotency work.
- 11:00–11:30: Help Mom with bathroom + light exercises.
- 11:30–12:00: Take daily photo of left hip incision for Swedish (doc wants these through mid-Feb 2026); upload.
- 12:00–12:30: Lunch for both of us.
- 12:30–3:00: Feature work / meetings.
- 3:00–6:00: Home health aide on duty (starting week of 02/03/2026). Use this window for deeper focus, but stay available if aide has questions.

Reminders:
- Keep phone on loud in case Swedish cardiology nurse line calls back re: 01/28 BP crash.
- Oxycodone doses every 4 hours – alarms set.
- If I have to drop from a meeting suddenly (BP issues, shower help, etc.), update Teams status afterward.
- Don’t forget to lock laptop whenever stepping away – privacy screen on.

Goal: Treat this like a normal office day with a few 10–15 min caregiving interruptions instead of the other way around.

artifacts.content:
Subject: Portland trip for Sofia – checking in on coverage here

Hi Mom,

Just wanted to write this out so you’re not worried when I’m in Portland next month.

Isabel asked if I could come down March 10–24 to help with Sofia’s post-surgery recovery. The plan is that I’ll stay at their house in Portland for those two weeks, work remotely from their guest room, and tag-team with Isa and Daniel on school runs, meds, and keeping Sofia entertained while she’s stuck at home. Her surgery is at Doernbecher, and they’re expecting she’ll be pretty wiped out that first week.

For you, nothing changes with the Swedish restrictions – the surgeon’s 1/22 note still says you shouldn’t be alone for more than 30 minutes at a time for 4–6 weeks, so until they clear you, I’m going to treat that as gospel. By the time I go to Portland, the home health aide should already be in a good rhythm with the 3–6 p.m. window at Harbor, and I’ll keep paying her out of pocket even while I’m away. Between that, your January co-pays, and the aide deposit, I’m already around $3,200 on my credit card, but it’s worth it if it keeps you safe.

While I’m gone, Emily has offered to come by a couple of evenings each week to check on you and Oscar will keep your lap warm when you’re here. I’ll still call every day and help manage appointments and the oxy schedule from afar.

Love you,
Michael

metadata.subject: Portland trip for Sofia – checking in on coverage here

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Ramirez <mramirez.seattle@gmail.com>
To: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@northbridgetech.com>
Cc: HR Benefits <hr@northbridgetech.com>
Date: Mon, Jan 27, 2025 9:14 AM
Subject: Request for temporary remote work while caring for Mom during her chemotherapy at Swedish Medical Center (Feb–Apr 2025)

Hi Daniel,

I wanted to give you a heads-up about a family medical situation and formally request a temporary caregiving arrangement that would adjust how I’m working over the next few months.

My mom, Elena Sofia Ramirez, is starting chemotherapy treatment in early February 2025 at Swedish Medical Center – First Hill, Seattle. Her oncologist has laid out an intensive schedule that will run into April 2025, with multiple full-day infusion visits and follow-up labs. Because she lives alone at her condo on Harbor Ave SW and doesn’t have anyone else nearby who can consistently drive her and stay with her during and after chemo, I’ll be her primary support.

I’m requesting approval to work fully remote from February through April while I’m caring for her, including days when I’ll be at Swedish during her chemotherapy treatment. Practically, this would mean I’ll bring my laptop and hotspot to the infusion center and work from there when she’s stable, then finish the day from her condo. I’ll keep my normal 9–5:30 schedule, stay available on Teams, and continue to own my Payments & Billing deliverables.

If needed, I’m happy to document this as a formal temporary caregiving arrangement with HR and share appointment dates as they’re confirmed.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Request for temporary remote work while caring for Mom during her chemotherapy at Swedish Medical Center (Feb–Apr 2025)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Ramirez <mramirez.seattle@gmail.com>
To: Emily Grace Chen <echen.personal@gmail.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 6, 2025 7:42 PM
Subject: Lining up remote days for Dad, Emily’s therapy, and Mom’s dialysis through June

Hey Em,

Trying to get all the moving pieces in one place so we don’t go nuts.

First, Dad: after his stroke, his neurologist at Tacoma General Hospital wants him in post-stroke rehabilitation there and at his father’s apartment in Tacoma, WA for at least 3–4 days per week through June 2025. I’m going to ask NorthBridge for explicit flexibility to work remote those days so I can drive down early, get him to rehab, and then work from his place between sessions. That’s on top of the caregiving support I’m already giving Mom.

Second, you: I know Emily’s generalized anxiety flare-up has been rough. I’m planning to tell my manager I need to keep Tuesdays light so I can drive you to Harborview Medical Center – Behavioral Health Clinic, Seattle for your weekly 2-hour therapy sessions. With the ongoing Tuesdays, 3–5pm, Spring 2025 outpatient therapy block, I’ll shift standups or join by phone when needed.

Third, Mom: her late-stage kidney failure means she’s starting three 4-hour dialysis treatments at DaVita West Seattle Dialysis Center, Mondays/Wednesdays/Fridays 6–10am, ongoing as of March 2025. I’ll do early mornings there, then log in from her place or home.

It’s a lot, but if I can lock in remote work flexibility around all this, I think we can make it sustainable.

Love,
M

metadata.subject: Lining up remote days for Dad, Emily’s therapy, and Mom’s dialysis through June

Hi Mom and Dad,

Quick update on plans for March. Isabel and Daniel asked if I could come down for a two-week stay to help with Sofia’s post-surgery recovery after her procedure at Doernbecher Children’s Hospital. So I booked train tickets and I’ll be staying at my sister’s house in Portland, OR from March 10–24, 2025.

The idea is that I’ll help Isabel Ramirez-Lee and Daniel Lee with the temporary caregiving support: school drop-offs for Sofia Elena Lee once she’s cleared, meds and ice packs, and just keeping her entertained while she’s stuck at home. I’ll set up my laptop in their guest room so I can keep working Seattle hours and do partial remote work from there. My manager is on board as long as I’m online for standup and available for Payments & Billing reviews.

During that two-week stay, I’ll still be checking in with you both by video. Mom, we’ll make sure your appointments are covered; Dad, I’ll coordinate with you around your Tacoma schedule so you’re not on your own.

I’ll send my full travel details once I have the confirmation email.

Love,
Michael
